name: Anthropic Skills
description: Official collection of Agent Skills from Anthropic, including document and example skills.
longDescription: The first-party Skills repository from Anthropic. Contains document skills (docx, pdf, pptx, xlsx) and example skills that demonstrate the Agent Skills format. A canonical reference for how a well-formed SKILL.md is structured and how progressive disclosure works in practice.
author: anthropics

install.claude: Clone into .claude/skills/, or browse the repo and copy the SKILL.md folder you want.

name: Claude Plugins (Official)
description: Anthropic's official marketplace of plugins and skills for Claude Code.
longDescription: The official plugin marketplace for Claude Code. Add it as a marketplace, then install individual plugins and skills directly from the CLI. First-party, maintained by Anthropic.
author: anthropics

install.claude: /plugin marketplace add anthropics/claude-plugins-official  then  /plugin install <plugin-name>

name: Superpowers
description: A battle-tested set of skills for TDD, debugging, and human-AI collaboration.
longDescription: Superpowers is a community collection of skills covering test-driven development, systematic debugging, and effective collaboration workflows. Designed to make a coding agent more rigorous and methodical rather than just faster.
author: obra

install.claude: /plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers  then  /plugin install superpowers  — or clone into .claude/skills/.

name: Awesome Claude Skills
description: A curated, community-maintained list of Claude skills and resources.
longDescription: A meta entry: a curated 'awesome list' of Claude skills, plugins, and learning resources. Start here to discover skills across the ecosystem before drilling into individual repos.
author: travisvn

install.claude: This is a curated list, not an installable skill — browse the README and follow links to individual skills.

name: Awesome Cursor Rules
description: The most starred collection of Cursor rule files for every major language, framework, and stack.
longDescription: The go-to Cursor rules repository with over 40k stars. Contains ready-made .cursorrules and MDC configurations for TypeScript/React, Python, Go, Rust, Next.js, Vue, and dozens more stacks. Each file encodes conventions, style guides, and agent behavior tuning for a specific toolchain.
author: PatrickJS

install.cursor: Browse `rules/` in the repo, find the file for your stack, and copy it into `.cursor/rules/` in your project root.

name: Devin Cursor Rules
description: Rules that give Cursor and Windsurf long-horizon planning and self-criticism, mimicking Devin-grade agents.
longDescription: A minimal but powerful set of rules that transforms Cursor and Windsurf into more deliberate, planning-first agents. Encodes planning phases, scratch-pad reasoning, and self-critique loops so the agent checks its own assumptions before writing code — dramatically improving output quality on complex multi-step tasks.
author: grapeot

install.cursor: Copy `.cursorrules` from the repo root into your project root.
install.windsurf: Copy `.cursorrules` from the repo and save it as `.windsurfrules` in your project root.

name: Claude Skills Collection
description: 337 skills and plugins for Claude Code and 8+ AI agents — engineering, product, compliance, and daily productivity.
longDescription: One of the largest community skill collections for Claude Code. Spans software engineering, marketing, product management, compliance (GDPR, HIPAA), C-level advisory, research, business operations, and productivity. Also includes Codex AGENTS.md files, Gemini GEMINI.md, and Cursor MDC rules for true multi-agent coverage.
author: alirezarezvani

install.claude: /plugin marketplace add alirezarezvani/claude-skills  then  /plugin install <skill-name>
install.codex: Copy the AGENTS.md file for your use case from the repo into your project root.
install.gemini: Copy the GEMINI.md file for your use case from the repo into your project root.
install.cursor: Copy the .mdc rule files from the cursor-rules/ folder into `.cursor/rules/` in your project.

name: Trail of Bits Security Skills
description: Professional security research skills from Trail of Bits — vulnerability detection, code audit, and hardening workflows.
longDescription: Claude Code skills authored by Trail of Bits, a leading security research firm. Covers SAST-style vulnerability detection, secure code review checklists, cryptographic pitfall detection, and structured audit workflows used by professional security researchers and penetration testers.
author: trailofbits

install.claude: /plugin marketplace add trailofbits/skills  then  /plugin install <skill-name>

name: Dev Browser
description: Claude skill that opens a real Playwright-powered browser so the agent can navigate, click, and test UIs end-to-end.
longDescription: Equips Claude Code with a live browser via Playwright. The agent can open URLs, interact with UI elements, take screenshots, fill forms, and verify behavior — enabling true end-to-end testing and visual debugging without leaving the coding session. Ideal for front-end and full-stack testing workflows.
author: SawyerHood

install.claude: /plugin marketplace add SawyerHood/dev-browser  then  /plugin install dev-browser

name: AI Prompts (instructa)
description: Curated AI prompts for Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, and GitHub Copilot — covering code quality, testing, and workflow rules.
longDescription: A curated set of battle-tested AI prompts packaged for four major agents. Covers code quality conventions, naming standards, testing guidance, and workflow automation rules. Each prompt is tuned for the specific context format of its target agent: MDC for Cursor, rules for Windsurf, instructions for Copilot, and prompts for Cline.
author: instructa

install.cursor: Copy .mdc files from the `cursor-rules/` folder into `.cursor/rules/` in your project.
install.windsurf: Append rule content from the `windsurf/` folder to `.windsurfrules` in your project root.
install.copilot: Copy content from the `github-copilot/` folder into `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.

name: AI Ready
description: Copilot CLI skill that analyzes any repo and auto-generates AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions, CI configs, and issue templates.
longDescription: A GitHub CLI extension that wires up AI-agent configuration for any repository in one command. Generates AGENTS.md (for Codex/Copilot), copilot-instructions.md, Claude skills, CI workflow files, and GitHub issue templates — making any project instantly AI-agent-ready with minimal manual configuration.
author: johnpapa

install.copilot: Install the GitHub CLI extension: `gh extension install johnpapa/ai-ready` then run `gh ai-ready` in your repo.
install.claude: Run the tool to generate CLAUDE.md and skill stubs, then move them into `.claude/skills/`.
install.codex: Run the tool to generate AGENTS.md; place the output in your project root.

name: MCP Reference Servers
description: Official reference MCP server implementations for filesystems, Git, databases, cloud services, and DevOps tooling.
longDescription: The canonical MCP server collection from the Model Context Protocol project. Includes servers for local filesystems, Git operations, PostgreSQL, SQLite, GitHub, Brave Search, Google Drive, and more. Connect Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or Continue to external tools and infrastructure in a structured, tool-use-native way.
author: modelcontextprotocol

install.claude: Add a server via `claude mcp add <server-name> <command>` or add entries to `.mcp.json` in your project.
install.cursor: Configure servers in Cursor Settings → Model Context Protocol.
install.windsurf: Configure servers in Windsurf Settings → MCP Servers.
install.continue: Add MCP server entries to `.continue/config.yaml` under the `mcpServers` key.

name: Awesome MCP Servers for DevOps
description: Curated DevOps-focused MCP server list covering IaC, Kubernetes, CI/CD, cloud providers, and observability.
longDescription: A handpicked directory of production-ready MCP servers for DevOps engineers. Covers infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, Pulumi), Kubernetes operators, CI/CD integrations (GitHub Actions, GitLab, Jenkins), cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure), observability platforms (Datadog, Grafana), and security scanners — all vetted for maintenance quality.
author: WagnerAgent

install.claude: Browse the list, pick a DevOps MCP server, and install it with `claude mcp add <server-name> npx <package>`.
install.cursor: Browse the list and configure your chosen server in Cursor Settings → Model Context Protocol.
install.windsurf: Browse the list and configure your chosen server in Windsurf Settings → MCP Servers.

name: GitHub Copilot CLI
description: GitHub Copilot in your terminal — suggests shell commands, explains pipelines, and automates DevOps workflows from the CLI.
longDescription: The official GitHub CLI extension for Copilot. Provides `gh copilot suggest` (generate a shell command from plain English) and `gh copilot explain` (decode any shell command or pipeline). Invaluable for DevOps engineers who live in the terminal and want Copilot assistance without leaving the shell.
author: github

install.copilot: Install the GitHub CLI if needed, then: `gh extension install github/copilot-cli`. Use with `gh copilot suggest` and `gh copilot explain`.

name: Gemini Cookbook
description: Official Gemini API cookbook with Jupyter notebooks for data analysis, multimodal reasoning, and code execution.
longDescription: Google's official collection of Gemini API examples. Covers text and multimodal generation, audio/video analysis, code execution, function calling, document understanding, and data analysis pipelines. Each notebook is a self-contained, runnable example with clear explanations. The definitive starting point for Gemini-powered data applications.
author: google-gemini

install.gemini: Clone the repo and open notebooks in Google Colab. Extract reusable prompt patterns into a GEMINI.md in your project root to guide the Gemini CLI on similar tasks.

name: Claude Cookbooks
description: Anthropic's official notebook collection showing effective Claude patterns for data extraction, RAG, pipelines, and automation.
longDescription: The official Anthropic cookbook of practical Claude examples in Jupyter notebook format. Covers summarization pipelines, RAG patterns, structured data extraction, code generation, tool use, and function calling. Each recipe is production-oriented and runnable. Extract patterns into CLAUDE.md or `.claude/skills/` to reuse them in your coding sessions.

install.claude: Clone and run notebooks locally or in Colab. Copy reusable prompt patterns into a skill in `.claude/skills/` or directly into CLAUDE.md.
